citations.desc: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC).
citations.narrative:
The IACUC conducted inspections of the animal facilities on March 24, 2025 and November 21, 2025. There was an 8
month span of time between the two inspections.
The IACUC must inspect the animal facilities, at least once every six months, in order to assure the proper housing and
general welfare of animals.
An inspection of the facility must be performed by the IACUC at least once every six months.
Correct by May 21, 2026.

citations.desc: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC).
citations.narrative:
The IACUC submitted semiannual reports of the facility inspections and program reviews to the Institutional Official (IO)
on April 3, 2025 and December 17, 2025. However, the reports to the IO did not contain signatures from the IACUC
members.
Reports submitted to the IO shall be reviewed and signed by a majority of the IACUC members to confirm agreement with
the inspection findings. Failure of a majority of the IACUC members to review the findings and sign the report could lead
to the omission of a minority view or a deficiency in the facility's animal use program.
Correct by having the semiannual reports reviewed and signed by a majority of the IACUC members before submission to
the IO.
Correct by April 8, 2026.

The facility submitted their annual report for the 2024 fiscal year on December 19, 2024, and their 2025 annual report on
January 7, 2026. Both annual reports were submitted after the required due date.
Failure to submit annual reports by the required submission date can make it difficult for APHIS to assess which animals
are being used for teaching, research, and testing and assure that the facility is adhering to the standards and regulations
under the Act.
Each reporting facility shall submit an annual report to the Deputy Administrator on or before December 1 of each
calendar year.
Corrected prior to the inspection on March 11, 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the attending veterinarian.
n

narrative:
2.31(c)(2) 
   
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC). 
The IACUC conducted inspections of the animal facilities on March 24, 2025 and November 21, 2025. There was an 8 
month span of time between the two inspections. 
The IACUC must inspect the animal facilities, at least once every six months, in order to assure the proper housing and 
general welfare of animals. 
An inspection of the facility must be performed by the IACUC at least once every six months. 
Correct by May 21, 2026. 
2.31(c)(3) 
   
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC). 
The IACUC submitted semiannual reports of the facility inspections and program reviews to the Institutional Official (IO) 
on April 3, 2025 and December 17, 2025. However, the reports to the IO did not contain signatures from the IACUC 
members. 
Reports submitted to the IO shall be reviewed and signed by a majority of the IACUC members to confirm agreement with 
the inspection findings. Failure of a majority of the IACUC members to review the findings and sign the report could lead 
to the omission of a minority view or a deficiency in the facility's animal use program. 
Correct by having the semiannual reports reviewed and signed by a majority of the IACUC members before submission to 
the IO. 
Correct by April 8, 2026. 
2.36(a) 
   
Annual report. 
The facility submitted their annual report for the 2024 fiscal year on December 19, 2024, and their 2025 annual report on 
January 7, 2026. Both annual reports were submitted after the required due date.  
Failure to submit annual reports by the required submission date can make it difficult for APHIS to assess which animals 
are being used for teaching, research, and testing and assure that the facility is adhering to the standards and regulations 
under the Act. 
Each reporting facility shall submit an annual report to the Deputy Administrator on or before December 1 of each

calendar year. 
Corrected prior to the inspection on March 11, 2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the attending veterinarian.
